scope_note: QA Witt Tower Eisenstein Form Real-Data Certificate [456]. Tests f(b,e)=b*b+b*e-e*e (Z[phi]/Witt norm) on real ^GSPC monthly rank-bin state pairs (N=299, same QA mapping as certs [453]-[455]). T-step identity: f(e,b+e)=-f(b,e) holds for ALL 299 pairs (algebraic guarantee, verified on actual integer bin values). Distribution: n_pos=207 (69.2%), n_neg=89 (29.8%), n_zero=3 (only at b=e=0 crash-cluster months). Sign-flip rate=50.7% (consistent with T-step alternation). f<0 transience: P(f_{t+1}<0|f_t<0)=13/88=14.8% (<=0.30) -- f<0 is as transient as S-orbit. PREDICTIVE NULL (key result): perm_p(two-tail)=0.752 -- f sign does NOT predict next-month returns. S-orbit f values: {-81, 0, 81, 324, 405} -- both signs present, orbit class does not determine Eisenstein sign. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) T-step identity for all N=299 pairs PASS. (C2) n_pos/N=69.2% in [0.55,0.80], n_zero=3<=5 PASS. (C3) sign-flip rate=50.7% in [0.40,0.60] PASS. (C4) P(f<0 persist)=14.8%<=0.30 PASS. (C5) perm_p=0.752>=0.10 (predictive null) PASS. (C6) S-orbit spans both Eisenstein signs PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541; cert [214] QA Eisenstein form algebraic identity. Structural parents: cert [110], cert [214], cert [453]. Validated 2026-06-18.
